steps.title: Identify what the passage says about hDNA
steps.content: The passage states that hDNA "offers unique benefits, such as access to genomic data from extirpated populations." The word "unique" signals that hDNA provides something other types of genomic data cannot — insights inaccessible from still-living populations.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — It may yield insights that other types of genomic data cannot.: directly supported by "unique benefits, such as access to genomic data from extirpated populations" — data unavailable from any living source. ✓
  • Choice B — It has thus far proved valuable mainly to researchers studying pathogens.: the passage describes one study on a fungal pathogen but does not claim hDNA is valuable mainly to pathogen researchers. ✗
  • Choice C — It may be underused because of its controversial status among scientists.: the passage attributes underuse to degradation problems, not controversy. ✗
  • Choice D — It tends to be much more degraded than other types of DNA of comparable age.: the passage says hDNA is often degraded but makes no comparison to other DNA types of similar age. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
